Our approach conventionally searches only for the “core” of the subgraph and then uses set-based methods to compute the number of occurrences that the “fringes” add. Our evaluation shows that Fringe-SGC is able to count the instances of many subgraphs that are too large for state-of-the-art SGC frameworks. Furthermore, Fringe-SGC running on a GPU outperforms the state-of-the-art GPU-based SGC frameworks by up to 20 × on average, especially on patterns with many fringes.
papers.abstract_zh: 子图计数 (SGC) 是许多重要应用的基本组成部分，包括网络安全、药物发现、社交网络分析和自然语言处理。然而，当前的 SGC 方法只能处理非常小的模式（也称为子图），因为计算负载随着模式的大小呈指数级增加。为了克服某些模式的这一限制，我们引入了一种称为 Fringe-SGC 的新技术和算法，用于计算子图在较大图中出现的确切次数。我们的方法通常只搜索子图的“核心”，然后使用基于集合的方法来计算“边缘”添加的出现次数。我们的评估表明，Fringe-SGC 能够计算许多对于最先进的 SGC 框架来说太大的子图的实例。此外，在 GPU 上运行的 Fringe-SGC 平均性能比最先进的基于 GPU 的 SGC 框架高出 20 倍，特别是在具有许多条纹的图案上。

papers.title: From Edge to HPC: Investigating Cross-Facility Data Streaming Architectures.
papers.abstract: In this paper, we investigate three cross-facility data streaming architectures, Direct Streaming (DTS), Proxied Streaming (PRS), and Managed Service Streaming (MSS). We examine their architectural variations in data flow paths and deployment feasibility, and detail their implementation using the Data Streaming to HPC (DS2HPC) architectural framework and the SciStream memory-to-memory streaming toolkit on the production-grade Advanced Computing Ecosystem (ACE) infrastructure at Oak Ridge Leadership Computing Facility (OLCF). We present a workflow-specific evaluation of these architectures using three synthetic workloads derived from the streaming characteristics of scientific workflows. Through simulated experiments, we measure streaming throughput, round-trip time, and overhead under work sharing, work sharing with feedback, and broadcast and gather messaging patterns commonly found in AI-HPC communication motifs. Our study shows that DTS offers a minimal-hop path, resulting in higher throughput and lower latency, whereas MSS provides greater deployment feasibility and scalability across multiple users but incurs significant overhead. PRS lies in between, offering a scalable architecture whose performance matches DTS in most cases.
papers.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们研究了三种跨设施数据流架构：直接流（DTS）、代理流（PRS）和托管服务流（MSS）。我们检查了它们在数据流路径和部署可行性方面的架构变化，并详细介绍了它们在橡树岭领先计算设施 (OLCF) 的生产级高级计算生态系统 (ACE) 基础设施上使用 Data Streaming to HPC (DS2HPC) 架构框架和 SciStream 内存到内存流工具包的实现。我们使用源自科学工作流程的流特征的三个合成工作负载，对这些架构进行了特定于工作流程的评估。通过模拟实验，我们测量了工作共享、带反馈的工作共享以及广播和收集 AI-HPC 通信主题中常见的消息传递模式下的流吞吐量、往返时间和开销。我们的研究表明，DTS 提供了最小跳数路径，从而实现更高的吞吐量和更低的延迟，而 MSS 提供了跨多个用户的更大的部署可行性和可扩展性，但会产生大量开销。 PRS 介于两者之间，提供可扩展的架构，其性能在大多数情况下与 DTS 相匹配。

papers.title: From Exploration to Explanation: ML-Driven Causal Discovery for Datacenter Reliability at Scale.
papers.abstract: Modern datacenters operate at unprecedented scale, supporting HPC and AI workloads while consuming hundreds of megawatts of power. Their reliability is challenged by complex interdependencies across cooling, power, and network subsystems, where failures can cascade into downtime and degraded performance. Existing monitoring approaches, largely threshold or only correlation-based, struggle to isolate root causes within high-dimensional, evolving telemetry. We present PACE (Pattern and Causal Exploration), an ML-based framework that combines unsupervised correlation clustering with supervised, lag-aware Granger causality to uncover subsystem structure and directed causal pathways from multivariate telemetry. PACE yields interpretable causal graphs, subsystem heatmaps, that align with physical processes and control logic, providing actionable insights for operations. Finally, we discuss how embedding PACE into digital twin architectures enables causal-informed what-if reasoning, advancing the reliability and efficiency in datacenters.
papers.abstract_zh: 现代数据中心以前所未有的规模运行，支持 HPC 和 AI 工作负载，同时消耗数百兆瓦的电力。它们的可靠性受到冷却、电源和网络子系统之间复杂的相互依赖性的挑战，其中故障可能会导致停机和性能下降。现有的监控方法（主要是基于阈值或仅基于相关性）很难在高维、不断发展的遥测中隔离根本原因。我们提出了 PACE（模式和因果探索），这是一个基于机器学习的框架，它将无监督相关聚类与有监督、滞后感知的格兰杰因果关系相结合，以揭示子系统结构和来自多元遥测的定向因果路径。 PACE 生成可解释的因果图、子系统热图，与物理过程和控制逻辑保持一致，为操作提供可操作的见解。最后，我们讨论如何将 PACE 嵌入到数字孪生架构中，从而实现基于因果关系的假设推理，从而提高数据中心的可靠性和效率。

papers.title: From Path-Aware to Application-Aware Source Routing using Traffic Classes.
papers.abstract: As one of the most complex challenges in networking, traffic engineering (TE) involves difficult decisions on how to do capacity planning, allocate flows, or even split traffic among multiple paths, in order to minimize congestion. Where creating an optimized solution is cumbersome and difficult, researchers have turned to AI/ML techniques to enable the network to learn based on traffic characteristics and network patterns, making optimal decisions. SDN-based TE tools, such as Teal, Hecate, and B4, among others, have utilized classification or deep reinforcement learning to identify optimal TE solutions. The additional advent of source routing tools, such as PolKA, has introduced the capability for finer-grained control of flows. In this paper, we explore the concept of traffic classification at the edge, enabling source routing to perform flow allocation based on incoming flow requests. We develop new traffic class patterns and queuing methods to ensure fair queuing allocation, thereby satisfying all traffic classes and achieving an optimized network utilization and allocation scheme. This work contributes to in-band network telemetry (INT) and P4 programmability, demonstrating how source routing can enable finer-grained decision-making for self-engineering networks.
papers.abstract_zh: 作为网络中最复杂的挑战之一，流量工程 (TE) 涉及如何进行容量规划、分配流量，甚至在多条路径之间分割流量以最大限度地减少拥塞等方面的艰难决策。在创建优化解决方案既麻烦又困难的情况下，研究人员转向人工智能/机器学习技术，使网络能够根据流量特征和网络模式进行学习，从而做出最佳决策。基于 SDN 的 TE 工具（例如 Teal、Hecate 和 B4 等）已利用分类或深度强化学习来识别最佳 TE 解决方案。 PolKA 等源路由工具的出现引入了更细粒度的流量控制功能。在本文中，我们探讨了边缘流量分类的概念，使源路由能够根据传入流请求执行流分配。我们开发新的流量类别模式和排队方法，以确保公平的排队分配，从而满足所有流量类别并实现优化的网络利用率和分配方案。这项工作有助于带内网络遥测 (INT) 和 P4 可编程性，展示源路由如何为自工程网络实现更细粒度的决策。

papers.title: From Soil to Software: Experience from a STEM Workshop on Smart Plant Care and Teachable Machines.
papers.abstract: In this paper, we present our work on developing a sensor-based device capable of large-scale environmental data collection. We also outline how we integrated this technology into a STEM education workshop for high school students. Conducted over three consecutive days as part of the Toledo EXCEL program, the workshop aimed to introduce students to foundational computing concepts, including data acquisition, machine learning, and artificial intelligence through accessible, hands-on activities. Participants used a custom-built IoT sensor system in conjunction with visual programming tools like MakeCode to create a smart plant care assistant. They also explored basic machine learning by training classifiers using Teachable Machine. We describe both the technical development of the sensor device and its role in engaging students with real-world computing applications. Finally, we outline plans to enhance future workshops with topics such as parallel computing and real-time data visualization dashboards.
papers.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们介绍了我们在开发能够大规模环境数据收集的基于传感器的设备方面的工作。我们还概述了如何将这项技术集成到面向高中生的 STEM 教育研讨会中。作为托莱多 EXCEL 计划的一部分，该研讨会连续举办了三天，旨在通过易于理解的实践活动向学生介绍基础计算概念，包括数据采集、机器学习和人工智能。参与者使用定制的物联网传感器系统结合 MakeCode 等可视化编程工具来创建智能植物护理助手。他们还通过使用 Teachable Machine 训练分类器来探索基本的机器学习。我们描述了传感器设备的技术发展及其在吸引学生参与现实世界计算应用方面的作用。最后，我们概述了加强未来研讨会的计划，主题包括并行计算和实时数据可视化仪表板等。

papers.title: FT-Transformer: Resilient and Reliable Transformer with End-to-End Fault Tolerant Attention.
papers.abstract: Transformer models rely on High-Performance Computing (HPC) resources for inference, where soft errors are inevitable in large-scale systems, making the reliability of the model particularly critical. Existing fault tolerance frameworks for Transformers are designed at the operation level without architectural optimization, leading to significant computational and memory overhead, which in turn reduces protection efficiency and limits scalability to larger models. In this paper, we implement module-level protection for Transformers by treating the operations within the attention module as a single kernel and applying end-to-end fault tolerance. This method provides unified protection across multi-step computations, while achieving comprehensive coverage of potential errors in the nonlinear computations. For linear modules, we design a strided algorithm-based fault tolerance (ABFT) that avoids inter-thread communication. Experimental results show that our end-to-end fault tolerance achieves up to 7.56 × speedup over traditional methods with an average fault tolerance overhead of 13.9%.
papers.abstract_zh: Transformer 模型依赖高性能计算 (HPC) 资源进行推理，在大规模系统中软错误不可避免，因此模型的可靠性尤为关键。现有的 Transformers 容错框架是在操作层面设计的，没有进行架构优化，导致大量的计算和内存开销，进而降低了保护效率并限制了更大模型的可扩展性。在本文中，我们通过将注意力模块内的操作视为单个内核并应用端到端容错来实现 Transformer 的模块级保护。该方法提供了跨多步计算的统一保护，同时实现了非线性计算中潜在错误的全面覆盖。对于线性模块，我们设计了一种基于跨步算法的容错（ABFT），可以避免线程间通信。实验结果表明，我们的端到端容错能力比传统方法实现了高达 7.56 倍的加速，平均容错开销为 13.9%。

papers.title: FZModules: A Heterogeneous Computing Framework for Customizable Scientific Data Compression Pipelines.
papers.abstract: Modern scientific simulations and instruments generate data volumes that overwhelm memory and storage, throttling scalability. Lossy compression mitigates this by trading controlled error for reduced footprint and throughput gains, yet optimal pipelines are highly data and objective specific, demanding compression expertise. GPU compressors supply raw throughput but often hard‑code fused kernels that hinder rapid experimentation, and underperform in rate–distortion. We present FZModules, a heterogeneous framework for assembling error‑bounded custom compression pipelines from high‑performance modules through a concise extensible interface. We further utilize an asynchronous task-backed execution library that infers data dependencies, manages memory movement, and exposes branch and stage level concurrency for powerful asynchronous compression pipelines. Evaluating three pipelines built with FZModules on four representative scientific datasets, we show they can compare end‑to‑end speedup of fused‑kernel GPU compressors while achieving similar rate–distortion to higher fidelity CPU or hybrid compressors, enabling rapid, domain-tailored design.
papers.abstract_zh: 现代科学模拟和仪器生成的数据量超出了内存和存储空间，限制了可扩展性。有损压缩通过用受控错误来减少占用空间和提高吞吐量来缓解这一问题，但最佳管道是高度数据和目标特定的、要求严格的压缩专业知识。 GPU 压缩器提供原始吞吐量，但通常会硬编码融合内核，这会阻碍快速实验，并且在速率失真方面表现不佳。我们提出了 FZModules，这是一个异构框架，用于通过简洁的可扩展接口从高性能模块组装误差有限的自定义压缩管道。我们进一步利用异步任务支持的执行库来推断数据依赖性、管理内存移动并为强大的异步压缩管道公开分支和阶段级并发性。通过在四个代表性科学数据集上评估使用 FZModules 构建的三个管道，我们发现它们可以比较融合内核 GPU 压缩器的端到端加速，同时实现与更高保真度 CPU 或混合压缩器类似的率失真，从而实现快速、定制的设计。

papers.title: Generating Frequently Asked Questions from Technical Support Tickets using Large Language Models.
papers.abstract: High-performance computing is a driving force behind scientific innovation and discovery. However, as the number of users and the complexity of high-performance computing systems grow, so does the volume and variability of technical issues handled by support teams. The evolving nature of these issues presents a need for automated tools that can extract clear, accurate, and relevant frequently asked questions directly from support tickets. This need was addressed by developing a novel pipeline that incorporates semantic clustering, representation learning, and large language models. While prior research laid strong foundations across classification, clustering and large language model-based questions & answers, our work augments these efforts by integrating semantic clustering, domain-specific summarization, and multi-stage generation into a scalable pipeline for autonomous technical support. To prioritize high-impact issues, the pipeline began by filtering tickets based on anomaly frequency and recency. It then leveraged an instruction-tuned large language model to clean and summarize each ticket into a structured issue-resolution pair. Next, unsupervised semantic clustering was performed to identify subclusters of semantically similar tickets within broader topic clusters. A large language model-based generation module was then applied to create frequently asked questions representing the most dominant issues. A structured evaluation by subject matter experts indicated that our approach transformed technical support tickets into understandable, factually sound, and pertinent frequently asked questions. The ability to extract fine-grained insights from raw ticket data enhances the scalability, efficiency, and responsiveness of technical support workflows in high-performance computing environments, ultimately enabling faster troubleshooting and more accessible pathways to scientific discovery.
papers.abstract_zh: 高性能计算是科学创新和发现背后的驱动力。然而，随着高性能计算系统的用户数量和复杂性的增加，支持团队处理的技术问题的数量和可变性也在增加。这些问题的不断发展的性质提出了对自动化工具的需求，这些工具可以直接从支持票证中提取清晰、准确且相关的常见问题。通过开发一种新颖的管道来解决这一需求，该管道结合了语义聚类、表示学习和大型语言模型。虽然之前的研究在分类、聚类和基于大型语言模型的问答方面奠定了坚实的基础，但我们的工作通过将语义聚类、特定领域摘要和多阶段生成集成到可扩展的管道中以提供自主技术支持来增强这些工作。为了优先考虑影响较大的问题，管道首先根据异常频率和新近度过滤票证。然后，它利用指令调整的大型语言模型来清理并将每张票证总结为结构化的问题解决对。接下来，执行无监督语义聚类，以识别更广泛的主题集群中语义相似的工单的子集群。然后应用基于大型语言模型的生成模块来创建代表最主要问题的常见问题。主题专家的结构化评估表明，我们的方法将技术支持请求转化为易于理解、事实合理且相关的常见问题。从原始票证数据中提取细粒度洞察的能力增强了高性能计算环境中技术支持工作流程的可扩展性、效率和响应能力，最终实现更快的故障排除和更容易的科学发现途径。

papers.title: Generating Permutations at Scale.
papers.abstract: In this paper, we introduce a new algorithm for generating large-scale permutations on distributed systems. Permutations get used in many applications, including statistical analysis, machine learning, sampling, graph neural networks, matching, crypto-analysis, and bootstrapping. In data science the permutation is also commonly referred to as a shuffle operation as it reorganizes elements in an entirely random manner by applying the permutation. Our algorithm is computationally efficient, easy to understand, and scales to large systems. We measure the performance of our new permutation generation scheme on a cluster of NVIDIA DGX-A100s, using up to 256 NVIDIA A100 GPUs. We show that we can generate a permutation of 137 billion values in approximately 1.1 seconds, with a throughput of 124 billion elements per second.
papers.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们介绍了一种在分布式系统上生成大规模排列的新算法。排列可用于许多应用，包括统计分析、机器学习、采样、图神经网络、匹配、密码分析和引导。在数据科学中，排列通常也称为洗牌操作，因为它通过应用排列以完全随机的方式重新组织元素。我们的算法计算效率高，易于理解，并且可以扩展到大型系统。我们使用多达 256 个 NVIDIA A100 GPU 在 NVIDIA DGX-A100 集群上测量了新的排列生成方案的性能。我们证明，我们可以在大约 1.1 秒内生成 1370 亿个值的排列，每秒吞吐量为 1240 亿个元素。

papers.title: Generative Latent Diffusion for Efficient Spatiotemporal Data Reduction.
papers.abstract: Generative models have demonstrated strong performance in conditional settings and can be viewed as a form of data compression, where the condition serves as a compact representation. However, their limited controllability and reconstruction accuracy restrict their practical application to data compression. In this work, we propose an efficient latent diffusion framework that bridges this gap by combining a variational autoencoder with a conditional diffusion model. Our method compresses only a small number of keyframes into latent space and uses them as conditioning inputs to reconstruct the remaining frames via generative interpolation, eliminating the need to store latent representations for every frame. This approach enables accurate spatiotemporal reconstruction while significantly reducing storage costs. Experimental results across multiple datasets show that our method achieves up to 10× higher compression ratios than rule-based state-of-the-art compressors such as SZ3, and up to 63% better performance than leading learning-based methods under the same reconstruction error.
papers.abstract_zh: 生成模型在条件设置中表现出了强大的性能，并且可以被视为数据压缩的一种形式，其中条件作为紧凑的表示。然而，它们有限的可控性和重构精度限制了它们在数据压缩中的实际应用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种有效的潜在扩散框架，通过将变分自动编码器与条件扩散模型相结合来弥补这一差距。我们的方法仅将少量关键帧压缩到潜在空间中，并使用它们作为条件输入，通过生成插值重建剩余帧，从而无需存储每个帧的潜在表示。这种方法可以实现准确的时空重建，同时显着降低存储成本。跨多个数据集的实验结果表明，我们的方法比基于规则的最先进压缩器（例如 SZ3）实现了高达 10 倍的压缩比，并且在相同的重建误差下比领先的基于学习的方法提高了高达 63% 的性能。

papers.title: gLLM: Global Balanced Pipeline Parallelism Systems for Distributed LLMs Serving with Token Throttling.
papers.abstract: Pipeline parallelism has emerged as a predominant approach for deploying large language models (LLMs) across distributed nodes, owing to its lower communication overhead compared to tensor parallelism. While demonstrating high throughput in request serving, pipeline parallelism often faces performance limitations caused by pipeline bubbles, which are primarily resulted from imbalanced computation delays across batches. Existing methods like Sarathi-Serve attempt to address this through hybrid scheduling of chunked prefill and decode tokens with a fixed token budget. However, such methods may still experience significant fluctuations, arising either from insufficient prefill tokens or uneven distribution of decode tokens, ultimately leading to computational imbalance.
papers.abstract_zh: 管道并行性已成为跨分布式节点部署大型语言模型 (LLM) 的主要方法，因为与张量并行性相比，其通信开销较低。在请求服务中展示高吞吐量的同时，管道并行性通常面临管道气泡引起的性能限制，这主要是由于批次之间的计算延迟不平衡造成的。 Sarathi-Serve 等现有方法尝试通过混合调度分块预填充和具有固定令牌预算的解码令牌来解决此问题。然而，由于预填充令牌不足或解码令牌分布不均匀，此类方法仍然可能会经历显着波动，最终导致计算不平衡。

papers.title: gParaKV: A GPGPU-accelerated Key-Value Separation-based KV Store with Optimized Compaction and Garbage Collection.
papers.abstract: LSM-tree-based key-value stores or KV stores are widely deployed in modern cloud storage systems thanks to high data storage efficiency and retrieval capabilities. The compaction process in the LSM-tree, however, results in severe performance bottlenecks, especially in scenarios involving large volumes of data. While key-value separation methods mitigate the performance bottlenecks caused by compaction, the existing methods do not fully address merge-sorting during compaction and expensive garbage collection (GC). We propose gParaKV, a GPGPU-empowered KV store with a KV separation mechanism, leveraging the GPGPU parallel technology to accelerate merge-sorting in compaction and GC. gParaKV embraces unique features like a GPGPU bitmap structure, parallel data marking, and a parallel GC mechanism. These critical components effectively curtail the overhead of merge-sorting and GC operations by virtue of parallel computing. We compare it with state-of-the-art KV stores (e.g., RocksDB, BlobDB, Wisckey, DiffKV, UniKV, and HPDK) under various workloads. The experimental results show that gParaKV can improve the write performance and GC efficiency compared to the existing key-value separation-based KV stores.
papers.abstract_zh: 基于LSM树的键值存储或KV存储由于高数据存储效率和检索能力而广泛部署在现代云存储系统中。然而，LSM-tree中的压缩过程会导致严重的性能瓶颈，尤其是在涉及大量数据的场景中。虽然键值分离方法减轻了压缩造成的性能瓶颈，但现有方法并没有完全解决压缩期间的合并排序和昂贵的垃圾收集（GC）问题。我们提出了 gParaKV，一种具有 KV 分离机制的 GPGPU 驱动的 KV 存储，利用 GPGPU 并行技术来加速压缩和 GC 中的合并排序。 gParaKV 拥有 GPGPU 位图结构、并行数据标记和并行 GC 机制等独特功能。这些关键组件通过并行计算有效地减少了合并排序和 GC 操作的开销。我们将其与各种工作负载下最先进的 KV 存储（例如 RocksDB、BlobDB、Wisckey、DiffKV、UniKV 和 HPDK）进行比较。实验结果表明，与现有基于键值分离的KV存储相比，gParaKV可以提高写入性能和GC效率。

papers.title: GPU Lossy Compression for HPC Can Be Versatile and Ultra-Fast.
papers.abstract: This work proposes VGC, a versatile and ultra-fast GPU lossy compression framework designed to address the growing data challenges in high-performance computing (HPC). VGC captures dimension information in scientific data and supports three compression algorithms, achieving high compression ratios across diverse HPC domains. Built with a highly optimized GPU kernel, VGC delivers state-of-the-art throughput with error control. In addition to compression ratio and speed, VGC supports two distinctive modes that enhance its versatility. Memory-efficient Compression uses a kernel fission design to compute compressed size, allocate only the required GPU memory, and compress data without waste, effectively reducing memory footprint. Selective Decompression introduces an early stopping mechanism that enables direct access to regions of interest without decompressing the entire dataset.
papers.abstract_zh: 这项工作提出了 VGC，这是一种多功能且超快速的 GPU 有损压缩框架，旨在解决高性能计算 (HPC) 中日益增长的数据挑战。 VGC捕获科学数据中的维度信息并支持三种压缩算法，在不同的HPC领域实现高压缩比。 VGC 采用高度优化的 GPU 内核构建，可提供最先进的吞吐量和错误控制。除了压缩比和速度之外，VGC 还支持两种独特的模式，增强了其多功能性。内存高效压缩采用内核裂变设计计算压缩大小，仅分配所需的GPU内存，压缩数据不浪费，有效减少内存占用。选择性解压缩引入了一种早期停止机制，可以直接访问感兴趣的区域，而无需解压缩整个数据集。

papers.title: GPU Programming for AI Workflow Development on AWS SageMaker: An Instructional Approach.
papers.abstract: We present the design, implementation, and comprehensive evaluation of a specialized course on GPU architecture, GPU programming, and how these are used for developing AI agents. This course is offered to undergraduate and graduate students during Fall 2024 and Spring 2025. The course began with foundational concepts in GPU/CPU hardware and parallel computing and progressed to develop RAG and optimizing them using GPUs. Students gained experience provisioning and configuring cloud-based GPU instances, implementing parallel algorithms, and deploying scalable AI solutions. We evaluated learning outcomes through assessments, course evaluations, and anonymous surveys. The results reveal that (1) AWS served as an effective and economical platform for practical GPU programming, (2) experiential learning significantly enhanced technical proficiency and engagement, and (3) the course strengthened students’ problem-solving and critical thinking skills through tools such as TensorBoard and HPC profilers, which exposed performance bottlenecks and scaling issues. Our findings underscore the pedagogical value of integrating parallel computing into STEM education. We advocate for broader adoption of similar electives across STEM curricula to prepare students for the demands of modern, compute-intensive fields.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们展示了关于 GPU 架构、GPU 编程以及如何使用它们开发 AI 代理的专业课程的设计、实现和综合评估。本课程在 2024 年秋季和 2025 年春季向本科生和研究生提供。本课程从 GPU/CPU 硬件和并行计算的基本概念开始，然后逐步开发 RAG 并使用 GPU 对其进行优化。学生获得了配置和配置基于云的 GPU 实例、实施并行算法以及部署可扩展 AI 解决方案的经验。我们通过评估、课程评估和匿名调查来评估学习成果。结果显示，(1) AWS 是实用 GPU 编程的有效且经济的平台，(2) 体验式学习显着提高了技术熟练程度和参与度，(3) 该课程通过 TensorBoard 和 HPC 分析器等工具增强了学生解决问题和批判性思维的能力，从而暴露了性能瓶颈和扩展问题。我们的研究结果强调了将并行计算融入 STEM 教育的教学价值。我们主张在 STEM 课程中更广泛地采用类似的选修课，为学生满足现代计算密集型领域的需求做好准备。

papers.title: Graphago: Accelerating SSD-based Graph Processing via Activity-Aware Graph Preprocessing.
papers.abstract: SSD-based graph processing systems have emerged as a cost-effective solution for handling the ever-growing, large-scale graphs that exceed the memory capacity of a single machine. However, the mismatch between the large SSD access granularity (e.g., 4KB) and the small size of the graph vertex data leads to significant read amplification and low I/O efficiency. Despite existing works proposing techniques like dynamic active data gathering or reordering-based graph preprocessing to tackle this challenge, they inevitably cause problems such as expensive on-line computation overheads, inefficient graph traversal, and I/O imbalance, thus degrading the performance of graph processing.
papers.abstract_zh: 基于 SSD 的图形处理系统已成为一种经济高效的解决方案，用于处理超出单机内存容量的不断增长的大规模图形。然而，大SSD访问粒度（例如4KB）与小图顶点数据之间的不匹配导致显着的读放大和低I/O效率。尽管现有的工作提出了动态主动数据收集或基于重新排序的图预处理等技术来应对这一挑战，但它们不可避免地会导致昂贵的在线计算开销、低效的图遍历和 I/O 不平衡等问题，从而降低图处理的性能。

papers.title: GreenMix: Energy-Efficient Serverless Computing via Randomized Sketching on Asymmetric Multi-Cores.
papers.abstract: GreenMix is motivated by the renewed interest in asymmetric multi-core processors and the emergence of the serverless computing model. Asymmetric multi-cores offer better energy and performance trade-offs by placing different core types on the same die. However, existing serverless scheduling techniques do not leverage these benefits. GreenMix is the first serverless work to reduce energy and serverless keep-alive costs while meeting QoS targets by leveraging asymmetric multi-cores. GreenMix employs randomized sketching, tailored for serverless execution and keep-alive, to perform within 10% of the optimal solution in terms of energy efficiency and keep-alive cost reduction. GreenMix’s effectiveness is demonstrated through evaluations on clusters of ARM big.LITTLE and Intel Alder Lake asymmetric processors. It outperforms competing state-of-the-art schedulers, offering a novel approach for energy-efficient serverless computing.
papers.abstract_zh: GreenMix 的动机是人们对非对称多核处理器重新产生兴趣以及无服务器计算模型的出现。非对称多核通过将不同的核心类型放置在同一芯片上，提供更好的能量和性能权衡。然而，现有的无服务器调度技术并没有利用这些优势。 GreenMix 是第一个无服务器工作，旨在通过利用非对称多核来降低能源和无服务器保持活动成本，同时满足 QoS 目标。 GreenMix 采用随机草图，专为无服务器执行和保持活动而定制，在能源效率和保持活动成本降低方面的执行效果与最佳解决方案相差 10% 以内。 GreenMix 的有效性通过对 ARM big.LITTLE 和 Intel Alder Lake 非对称处理器集群的评估得到证明。它的性能优于竞争对手最先进的调度程序，为节能无服务器计算提供了一种新颖的方法。

papers.title: GridMind: LLMs-Powered Agents for Power System Analysis and Operations.
papers.abstract: The complexity of traditional power system analysis workflows presents significant barriers to efficient decision-making in modern electric grids. This paper presents GridMind, a multi-agent AI system that integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with deterministic engineering solvers to enable conversational scientific computing for power system analysis. The system employs specialized agents coordinating AC Optimal Power Flow and N-1 contingency analysis through natural language interfaces while maintaining numerical precision via function calls. GridMind addresses workflow integration, knowledge accessibility, context preservation, and expert decision-support augmentation. Experimental evaluation on IEEE test cases demonstrates that the proposed agentic framework consistently delivers correct solutions across all tested language models, with smaller LLMs achieving comparable analytical accuracy with reduced computational latency. This work establishes agentic AI as a viable paradigm for scientific computing, demonstrating how conversational interfaces can enhance accessibility while preserving numerical rigor essential for critical engineering applications.
papers.abstract_zh: 传统电力系统分析工作流程的复杂性给现代电网的高效决策带来了重大障碍。本文介绍了 GridMind，这是一个多代理人工智能系统，它将大型语言模型 (LLM) 与确定性工程求解器集成在一起，以实现用于电力系统分析的对话式科学计算。该系统采用专门的代理，通过自然语言接口协调交流最佳潮流和 N-1 应急分析，同时通过函数调用保持数值精度。 GridMind 致力于工作流程集成、知识可访问性、上下文保存和专家决策支持增强。对 IEEE 测试用例的实验评估表明，所提出的代理框架在所有测试的语言模型中一致地提供了正确的解决方案，较小的法学硕士实现了可比的分析精度，同时减少了计算延迟。这项工作将代理人工智能建立为科学计算的可行范例，展示了对话界面如何增强可访问性，同时保持关键工程应用所必需的数字严谨性。

papers.title: Guiding Application Users via Estimation of Computational Resources for Massively Parallel Chemistry Computations.
papers.abstract: In this work, we develop machine learning (ML) based strategies to predict resources (costs) required for massively parallel chemistry computations to guide application users before they commit to running expensive experiments on a supercomputer. By predicting application execution time, we determine the optimal runtime parameter values such as number of nodes and tile sizes. Two key questions of interest to users are addressed. The first is the shortest-time question, where the user is interested in knowing the parameter values to achieve the shortest execution time for a given problem size and a target supercomputer. The second is the cheapest-run question in which the user is interested in minimizing resource costs, e.g., finding the number of nodes and tile size that minimizes the number of node-hours for a given problem size.
papers.abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们开发了基于机器学习 (ML) 的策略来预测大规模并行化学计算所需的资源（成本），从而在应用程序用户承诺在超级计算机上运行昂贵的实验之前为他们提供指导。通过预测应用程序执行时间，我们确定最佳运行时参数值，例如节点数量和图块大小。解决了用户感兴趣的两个关键问题。第一个是最短时间问题，用户有兴趣了解参数值，以实现给定问题大小和目标超级计算机的最短执行时间。第二个是最便宜的运行问题，其中用户对最小化资源成本感兴趣，例如，找到最小化给定问题大小的节点小时数的节点数量和图块大小。

papers.title: Hardware-Software Co-Design of Iterative Filter-Update Numerical Methods Using Processing-In-Memory.
papers.abstract: Data movement is a key bottleneck in applications such as machine learning and scientific computing. Some software techniques address this by computing on subsets of data but this still requires reading the entire dataset to determine the subset. We propose a hardware-software co-design approach for iterative methods centered around two operations–filtering and updating. We introduce a domain-specific language that supports these computational patterns to enable PIM programming. Since filter and update are simple pointwise operations, PIM hardware requires only limited compute capability.
papers.abstract_zh: 数据移动是机器学习和科学计算等应用程序的关键瓶颈。一些软件技术通过计算数据子集来解决这个问题，但这仍然需要读取整个数据集来确定子集。我们提出了一种硬件-软件协同设计方法，用于围绕两个操作（过滤和更新）的迭代方法。我们引入了一种支持这些计算模式的领域特定语言来实现 PIM 编程。由于过滤和更新是简单的逐点操作，因此 PIM 硬件仅需要有限的计算能力。

papers.title: HELM: Characterizing Unified Memory Accesses to Improve GPU Performance under Memory Oversubscription.
papers.abstract: Unified Memory (UM) technologies simplify memory management across CPU and GPU domains in GPU-accelerated heterogeneous architectures through transparent data migration. However, the default migration mechanism can severely degrade performance when applications oversubscribe GPU memory. Existing approaches to mitigating this performance degradation often fail to generalize, as they target specific application types, require specialized hardware, or integrate opaque classification methods.
papers.abstract_zh: 统一内存 (UM) 技术通过透明的数据迁移，简化了 GPU 加速异构架构中跨 CPU 和 GPU 域的内存管理。然而，当应用程序过度订阅 GPU 内存时，默认迁移机制可能会严重降低性能。缓解这种性能下降的现有方法通常无法推广，因为它们针对特定的应用程序类型，需要专门的硬件或集成不透明的分类方法。

papers.title: Hetis: Serving LLMs in Heterogeneous GPU Clusters with Fine-grained and Dynamic Parallelism.
papers.abstract: The significant resource demands in LLM serving prompts production clusters to fully utilize heterogeneous hardware by partitioning LLM models across a mix of high-end and low-end GPUs. However, existing parallelization approaches often struggle to scale efficiently in heterogeneous environments due to their coarse-grained and static parallelization strategies.
papers.abstract_zh: LLM 服务中的大量资源需求促使生产集群通过在高端和低端 GPU 的组合上划分 LLM 模型来充分利用异构硬件。然而，现有的并行化方法由于其粗粒度和静态并行化策略，通常难以在异构环境中有效扩展。

papers.title: High-Performance and Power-Efficient Emulation of Matrix Multiplication using INT8 Matrix Engines.
papers.abstract: Recent architectures integrate high-performance and power-efficient matrix engines. These engines demonstrate remarkable performance in low-precision matrix multiplication, which is crucial in deep learning. Several techniques have been proposed to emulate single- and double-precision general matrix-matrix multiplication (SGEMM and DGEMM, respectively) by leveraging such low-precision matrix engines. In this study, we present emulation methods that significantly outperforms conventional approaches. On a GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip, the proposed DGEMM emulation achieves a 1.4 × speedup and a 43% improvement in power efficiency compared to native DGEMM for sufficiently large problems. The proposed SGEMM emulation achieves a 3.0 × speedup and a 154% improvement in power efficiency compared to native SGEMM for sufficiently large problems. Furthermore, compared to conventional emulation methods, the proposed emulation achieves more than 2 × higher performance and superior power efficiency.
papers.abstract_zh: 最近的架构集成了高性能和高能效的矩阵引擎。这些引擎在低精度矩阵乘法方面表现出卓越的性能，这对于深度学习至关重要。已经提出了几种技术来利用此类低精度矩阵引擎来模拟单精度和双精度通用矩阵乘法（分别为 SGEMM 和 DGEMM）。在这项研究中，我们提出了明显优于传统方法的仿真方法。在 GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip 上，对于足够大的问题，与原生 DGEMM 相比，所提出的 DGEMM 仿真实现了 1.4 倍的加速和 43% 的功率效率提高。对于足够大的问题，与原生 SGEMM 相比，所提出的 SGEMM 仿真实现了 3.0 倍的加速和 154% 的功率效率提高。此外，与传统的仿真方法相比，所提出的仿真实现了 2 倍以上的更高性能和卓越的功效。

papers.title: High-Performance Branch-Free Algorithms for Extended-Precision Floating-Point Arithmetic.
papers.abstract: We present new branch-free algorithms for floating-point arithmetic at double, triple, or quadruple the native machine precision. These algorithms are the fastest known by at least an order of magnitude and are conjectured to be optimal, not only in an asymptotic sense, but in their exact FLOP count and circuit depth. Unlike previous algorithms, which either use complex branching logic or are only correct on specific classes of inputs, our algorithms have computer-verified proofs of correctness for all floating-point inputs within machine overflow and underflow thresholds. Compared to state-of-the-art multiprecision libraries, our algorithms achieve up to 11.7 × the peak performance of QD, 34.4 × over CAMPARY, 35.6 × over MPFR, and 41.4 × over FLINT.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们提出了新的无分支浮点算法，其精度是本机机器精度的两倍、三倍或四倍。这些算法是已知最快的至少一个数量级，并且被推测是最优的，不仅在渐近意义上，而且在其精确的 FLOP 计数和电路深度上。与以前的算法不同，以前的算法要么使用复杂的分支逻辑，要么仅在特定类别的输入上正确，而我们的算法对机器上溢和下溢阈值内的所有浮点输入具有经过计算机验证的正确性证明。与最先进的多精度库相比，我们的算法实现了 QD 峰值性能的 11.7 倍，是 CAMPARY 的 34.4 倍，是 MPFR 的 35.6 倍，是 FLINT 的 41.4 倍。

papers.title: How effective is matrix reordering for improving performance of sparse matrix-vector multiplication?
papers.abstract: This work evaluates the impact of matrix reordering on the performance of sparse matrix-vector multiplication across different multicore CPU platforms. Reordering can enhance performance by optimizing the non-zero element patterns to reduce total data movement and improve the load-balancing. We examine how these gains vary over different CPUs for different reordering strategies, focusing on both sequential and parallel execution. We address multiple aspects, including appropriate measurement methodology, comparison across different kinds of reordering strategies, consistency across machines, and impact of load imbalance.
papers.abstract_zh: 这项工作评估了矩阵重新排序对不同多核 CPU 平台上稀疏矩阵向量乘法性能的影响。重新排序可以通过优化非零元素模式来减少总数据移动并改善负载平衡，从而提高性能。我们研究了这些增益在不同 CPU 上针对不同重新排序策略的变化情况，重点关注顺序执行和并行执行。我们解决了多个方面的问题，包括适当的测量方法、不同类型的重新排序策略之间的比较、机器之间的一致性以及负载不平衡的影响。

papers.title: HP-MDR: High-performance and Portable Data Refactoring and Progressive Retrieval with Advanced GPUs.
papers.abstract: Scientific applications produce vast amounts of data, posing grand challenges in the underlying data management and analytic tasks. Progressive compression is a promising way to address this problem, as it allows for on-demand data retrieval with significantly reduced data movement cost. However, most existing progressive methods are designed for CPUs, leaving a gap for them to unleash the power of today’s heterogeneous computing systems with GPUs.In this work, we propose HP-MDR, a high-performance and portable data refactoring and progressive retrieval framework for GPUs. Our contributions are four-fold: (1) We carefully optimize the bitplane encoding and lossless encoding, two key stages in progressive methods, to achieve high performance on GPUs; (2) We propose pipeline optimization and incorporate it with data refactoring and progressive retrieval workflows to further enhance the performance for large data process; (3) We leverage our framework to enable high-performance data retrieval with guaranteed error control for common Quantities of Interest; (4) We evaluate HP-MDR and compare it with state of the arts using five real-world datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that HP-MDR delivers an average 13.68 × and 6.31 × throughput in data refactoring and progressive retrieval tasks, respectively. It also leads to 11.22 × throughput for recomposing required data representations under Quantity-of-Interest error control and 6.04 × performance for the corresponding end-to-end data retrieval, when compared with state-of-the-art solutions.
papers.abstract_zh: 科学应用程序会产生大量数据，给底层数据管理和分析任务带来巨大挑战。渐进式压缩是解决此问题的一种有前途的方法，因为它允许按需数据检索，并显着降低数据移动成本。然而，大多数现有的渐进方法都是为 CPU 设计的，这为它们利用 GPU 释放当今异构计算系统的能力留下了差距。在这项工作中，我们提出了 HP-MDR，一种用于 GPU 的高性能、可移植的数据重构和渐进检索框架。我们的贡献有四个方面：（1）我们仔细优化了位平面编码和无损编码（渐进式方法中的两个关键阶段），以在 GPU 上实现高性能； （2）我们提出管道优化，并将其与数据重构和渐进检索工作流程相结合，以进一步提高大数据处理的性能； (3) 我们利用我们的框架来实现高性能数据检索，并保证对常见感兴趣数量的错误控制； (4) 我们评估 HP-MDR 并使用五个真实数据集将其与现有技术进行比较。实验结果表明，HP-MDR 在数据重构和渐进式检索任务中的平均吞吐量分别为 13.68 倍和 6.31 倍。与最先进的解决方案相比，在兴趣数量错误控制下重构所需数据表示的吞吐量提高了 11.22 倍，相应的端到端数据检索的性能提高了 6.04 倍。

papers.title: HPC Digital Twins for Evaluating Scheduling Policies, Incentive Structures and their Impact on Power and Cooling.
papers.abstract: Schedulers are critical for optimal resource utilization in high-performance computing. Traditional methods to evaluate schedulers are limited to post-deployment analysis, or simulators, which do not model associated infrastructure. In this work, we present the first-of-its-kind integration of scheduling and digital twins in HPC. This enables what-if studies to understand the impact of parameter configurations and scheduling decisions on the physical assets, even before deployment, or regarching changes not easily realizable in production. We (1) provide the first digital twin framework extended with scheduling capabilities, (2) integrate various top-tier HPC systems given their publicly available datasets, (3) implement extensions to integrate external scheduling simulators. Finally, we show how to (4) implement and evaluate incentive structures, as-well-as (5) evaluate machine learning based scheduling, in such novel digital-twin based meta-framework to prototype scheduling. Our work enables what-if scenarios of HPC systems to evaluate sustainability, and the impact on the simulated system.
papers.abstract_zh: 调度程序对于高性能计算中的最佳资源利用至关重要。评估调度程序的传统方法仅限于部署后分析或模拟器，它们不会对相关基础设施进行建模。在这项工作中，我们首次在 HPC 中实现了调度和数字孪生的集成。这使得假设研究能够了解参数配置和调度决策对物理资产的影响，甚至在部署或重新研究生产中不易实现的更改之前也是如此。我们 (1) 提供第一个具有调度功能的数字孪生框架，(2) 集成各种顶级 HPC 系统（鉴于其公开可用的数据集），(3) 实现扩展以集成外部调度模拟器。最后，我们展示了如何（4）实施和评估激励结构，以及（5）评估基于机器学习的调度，在这种新颖的基于数字孪生的元框架中进行原型调度。我们的工作使 HPC 系统的假设场景能够评估可持续性以及对模拟系统的影响。

papers.title: HPC-R1: Characterizing R1-like Large Reasoning Models on HPC.
papers.abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) are becoming increasingly popular as they offer advanced capabilities in logical inference, mathematical reasoning, and knowledge synthesis, even beyond those of standard language models. However, their complex training workflows present significant challenges in reproducibility, efficiency, and system-level optimization. This paper introduces HPC-R1, a comprehensive characterization of LRM training on the NERSC Perlmutter supercomputer, representing behavior on a Top500-ranked system. We analyze all major stages, including supervised fine-tuning (SFT), Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)-based reinforcement learning (RL), autoregressive generation, and distillation using customized state-of-the-art frameworks. Our detailed performance analysis reveals key system inefficiencies and scaling behaviors. Through our in-depth analysis, we present 19 key observations across all stages, including 4 for SFT, 7 for GRPO-based RL, 6 for generation, and 2 for distillation. Based on these findings, we present several key recommendations to guide future HPC-AI system design.
papers.abstract_zh: 大型推理模型 (LRM) 变得越来越受欢迎，因为它们提供了逻辑推理、数学推理和知识合成方面的高级功能，甚至超越了标准语言模型。然而，其复杂的训练工作流程在可重复性、效率和系统级优化方面提出了重大挑战。本文介绍了 HPC-R1，它是 NERSC Perlmutter 超级计算机上 LRM 训练的综合表征，代表了 Top500 排名系统的行为。我们分析所有主要阶段，包括监督微调 (SFT)、基于组相对策略优化 (GRPO) 的强化学习 (RL)、自回归生成和使用定制的最先进框架的蒸馏。我们详细的性能分析揭示了关键的系统效率低下和扩展行为。通过深入分析，我们提出了各个阶段的 19 个关键观察结果，其中 4 个用于 SFT，7 个用于基于 GRPO 的 RL，6 个用于生成，2 个用于蒸馏。基于这些发现，我们提出了几项关键建议来指导未来的 HPC-AI 系统设计。

papers.title: HStencil: Matrix-Vector Stencil Computation with Interleaved Outer Product and MLA.
papers.abstract: Stencil computations are fundamental to various HPC and intelligent computing applications, often consuming significant execution time. The emergence of specialized matrix units presents new opportunities to accelerate stencil computations. While scalable matrix compute units provide substantial computing horsepower, prior efforts fail to fully utilize the computing capabilities for stencils due to suboptimal matrix-unit utilization, limited instruction-level parallelism, and low cache hit rates. This paper introduces HStencil, a novel stencil computing framework utilizing matrix and vector units. HStencil addresses these challenges through three contributions: 1) microkernels that jointly leverage matrix and vector units to enhance hardware utilization; 2) fine-grained instruction scheduling with interleaved execution to enhance instruction-level parallelism; and 3) spatial prefetch to sustain high performance when working sets exceed cache capacity. Evaluations on representative benchmarks demonstrate that HStencil achieves maximum speedups of 1.81x – 5.76x over auto-vectorization across different CPU platforms, delivers 31% - 91% higher performance versus state-of-the-art methods.
papers.abstract_zh: 模板计算是各种 HPC 和智能计算应用程序的基础，通常会消耗大量的执行时间。专用矩阵单元的出现为加速模板计算提供了新的机会。虽然可扩展矩阵计算单元提供了强大的计算能力，但由于矩阵单元利用率不理想、指令级并行性有限以及缓存命中率低，先前的努力未能充分利用模板的计算能力。本文介绍了 HStencil，一种利用矩阵和向量单元的新型模板计算框架。 HStencil 通过三个贡献来应对这些挑战：1）联合利用矩阵和向量单元来提高硬件利用率的微内核； 2）采用交错执行的细粒度指令调度，增强指令级并行性； 3) 空间预取，以在工作集超出缓存容量时维持高性能。对代表性基准的评估表明，HStencil 在不同 CPU 平台上比自动矢量化实现了 1.81 倍至 5.76 倍的最大加速，与最先进的方法相比，性能提高了 31% - 91%。

papers.title: Hybrid GPU Programming Education with Python and C++: Preferences, Performance and Common Python Pitfalls.
papers.abstract: We present a hybrid GPU programming curriculum that allows students to choose between Python (Numba) and C++ (CUDA), supporting language flexibility in a distance-learning context. The dual-language design aims to engage both students familiar scripting languages like Python and those experienced compiled languages like C/C++. The course was evaluated with 19 computer science students at Bachelor’s and Master’s levels. A pre-course survey assessed prior knowledge in programming and related tools. Students generally preferred the language they were more familiar with, and performance correlated with this prior experience. Although C/C++ users achieved slightly higher scores, regression analysis indicates that differences were largely due to prior knowledge, not language choice. Finally, we analyzes Python-specific pitfalls, including boundary errors, type mismatches in shared memory, and inefficient data transfers. These subtle issues often led to correctness or performance problems. We conclude with teaching recommendations to support pythonic GPU learning and help students avoid common mistakes.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们提供混合 GPU 编程课程，允许学生在 Python (Numba) 和 C++ (CUDA) 之间进行选择，支持远程学习环境中的语言灵活性。双语言设计旨在吸引学生熟悉的脚本语言（如Python）和经验丰富的编译语言（如C/C++）。该课程由 19 名计算机科学学士和硕士学生进行了评估。课程前调查评估了编程和相关工具的先验知识。学生通常更喜欢他们更熟悉的语言，并且表现与之前的经历相关。尽管 C/C++ 用户得分稍高，但回归分析表明差异主要是由于先验知识而不是语言选择造成的。最后，我们分析了 Python 特有的陷阱，包括边界错误、共享内存中的类型不匹配以及低效的数据传输。这些微妙的问题通常会导致正确性或性能问题。最后，我们提出了支持 Pythonic GPU 学习并帮助学生避免常见错误的教学建议。

papers.title: Hypertron: Efficiently Scaling Large Models by Exploring High-Dimensional Parallelization Space.
papers.abstract: Large models are evolving towards massive scale, diverse model architectures (dense and sparse) and long-context processing, which makes it very challenging to efficiently scale large models on parallel machines. The current widely-used parallelization strategies are often sub-optimal due to their limited parallelization strategy space. To this end, we propose Hypertron, a scalable parallel large-model training framework which incorporates an unprecedented high-dimensional (up to 7D) parallelization space, a holistic scheme for efficient dimension fusion, and a comprehensive performance model to guide the high-dimensional exploration. By exploiting the high-dimensional space to discover the optimal strategy which is not supported by existing frameworks, Hypertron significantly reduces memory and communication cost while improving parallel scalability. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that Hypertron achieves up to 56.7% Model FLOPs Utilization (MFU) on 2,048 new-generation Ascend NPU accelerators (scaling with supernodes) for different large models (such as sparse 141B and dense 310B), with 1.33x speedup over the best configuration of the state-of-the-art frameworks.
papers.abstract_zh: 大型模型正在朝着大规模、多样化模型架构（密集和稀疏）和长上下文处理的方向发展，这使得在并行机器上有效扩展大型模型变得非常具有挑战性。当前广泛使用的并行化策略由于并行化策略空间有限，往往不是最优的。为此，我们提出了Hypertron，一个可扩展的并行大模型训练框架，它结合了前所未有的高维（高达7D）并行化空间、高效维度融合的整体方案以及指导高维探索的综合性能模型。通过利用高维空间来发现现有框架不支持的最优策略，Hypertron 显着降低了内存和通信成本，同时提高了并行可扩展性。广泛的评估表明，Hypertron 在 2,048 个新一代 Ascend NPU 加速器（通过超级节点扩展）上针对不同的大型模型（例如稀疏 141B 和密集 310B）实现了高达 56.7% 的模型 FLOP 利用率（MFU），与最先进框架的最佳配置相比加速了 1.33 倍。

papers.title: HyTiS: Hybrid Tile Scheduling for GPU GEMM with Enhanced Wave Utilization and Cache Locality.
papers.abstract: General matrix-matrix multiplication (GEMM) is a fundamental operation in both deep learning and scientific computing. To accelerate these workloads, GPUs with a large number of streaming multiprocessors (SMs) are widely used. However, as modern GPUs scale in core count and adopt larger tile sizes, the wave quantization problem induced by partially filled waves results in growing hardware underutilization and substantially degraded performance. Existing solutions to this problem often suffer from low execution efficiency or introduce additional synchronization overhead.
papers.abstract_zh: 通用矩阵-矩阵乘法（GEMM）是深度学习和科学计算中的基本运算。为了加速这些工作负载，广泛使用具有大量流式多处理器 (SM) 的 GPU。然而，随着现代 GPU 增加核心数量并采用更大的块尺寸，部分填充的波引起的波量化问题导致硬件利用率不断提高，性能大幅下降。该问题的现有解决方案通常执行效率低或引入额外的同步开销。

papers.title: ILAN: The Interference- and Locality-Aware NUMA Scheduler.
papers.abstract: Modern HPC platforms increasingly adopt NUMA architectures, where OpenMP task-based programming model is a standard for enabling dynamic parallelism. However, the default OpenMP runtime is topology-agnostic, and the existing affinity policies are insufficient to ensure optimal performance on modern NUMA architectures. This lack of topology awareness results in suboptimal data locality and performance degradation. Additionally, the current OpenMP standard lacks mechanisms for detecting and mitigating the interference between concurrently executing tasks, further exacerbating the performance degradation. To enhance the performance of OpenMP task-based applications on NUMA architectures, we propose the ILAN scheduler: an interference- and locality-aware scheduler, employing moldability to dynamically minimize interference combined with hierarchical scheduling for improved data locality. We implement ILAN as an extension of LLVM OpenMP runtime. The results on a 64-core AMD Zen 4 platform show that ILAN achieves an average speedup of 13.2%, and a maximum speedup of 45.8% compared to the default scheduler.
papers.abstract_zh: 现代 HPC 平台越来越多地采用 NUMA 架构，其中 OpenMP 基于任务的编程模型是实现动态并行性的标准。然而，默认的 OpenMP 运行时与拓扑无关，现有的关联策略不足以确保现代 NUMA 架构上的最佳性能。缺乏拓扑意识会导致数据局部性不佳和性能下降。此外，当前的OpenMP标准缺乏检测和减轻并发执行任务之间干扰的机制，进一步加剧了性能下降。为了提高 NUMA 架构上 OpenMP 基于任务的应用程序的性能，我们提出了 ILAN 调度程序：一种干扰和局部感知调度程序，利用可塑性动态最小化干扰，并结合分层调度来改进数据局部性。我们将 ILAN 实现为 LLVM OpenMP 运行时的扩展。在64核AMD Zen 4平台上的结果显示，与默认调度器相比，ILAN平均加速13.2%，最大加速45.8%。

papers.title: Implementing Network-level QoS at HPC Datacenters to Enable Distributed Scientific Workflows.
papers.abstract: High Performance Computing (HPC) datacenters must handle real-time streams demanding sub-millisecond latency and bulk transfers requiring sustained multi-gigabit throughput—objectives that inherently compete on a shared network. Achieving both simultaneously requires end-to-end performance guarantees, typically provided by Quality of Service (QoS) that classify traffic, reserve bandwidth, and enforce priorities across every hop. While backbone and Wide Area Network (WAN) providers (e.g., ESnet) already enforce QoS, the local Ethernet ingress “last-mile” in HPC remains best-effort, creating a blind spot where latency accumulates and jeopardizes time-sensitive workflows. We fill this gap with standards-based Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) QoS solution on existing leaf–spine switches: packets are marked at the host, queued per class, and shaped on every last-mile hop through to the High Speed Network (HSN) gateway network interface card (NIC). We evaluate our solution in two scenarios representative of HPC traffic: Intra-domain—traffic that stays entirely within the datacenter (e.g., server-to-server flows over the leaf–spine Ethernet network), and Inter-domain—traffic that crosses the datacenter boundary (e.g., instrument Local Area Network (LAN) - WAN - HPC ingress). Our solution yields up to 60% more stable throughput, 30% fewer retransmissions, and tightly bounded jitter—deployable instantly on operational fabrics without hardware upgrades or proprietary extensions, and paving the way for true policy-driven, end-to-end performance in HPC datacenters.
papers.abstract_zh: 高性能计算 (HPC) 数据中心必须处理要求亚毫秒级延迟的实时流和需要持续数千兆位吞吐量的批量传输，这些目标本质上是在共享网络上竞争的。同时实现这两个目标需要端到端的性能保证，通常由服务质量 (QoS) 提供，服务质量 (QoS) 可以对流量进行分类、预留带宽并在每一跳上强制执行优先级。虽然骨干网和广域网 (WAN) 提供商（例如 ESnet）已经强制执行 QoS，但 HPC 中的本地以太网入口“最后一英里”仍然尽力而为，从而造成了延迟累积并危及时间敏感工作流程的盲点。我们在现有叶脊交换机上使用基于标准的差分服务代码点 (DSCP) QoS 解决方案来填补这一空白：数据包在主机上进行标记，按类别排队，并在到达高速网络 (HSN) 网关网络接口卡 (NIC) 的每个最后一英里跃点上进行整形。我们在代表 HPC 流量的两种场景中评估我们的解决方案：域内 — 完全保留在数据中心内的流量（例如，叶脊以太网上的服务器到服务器的流量），以及域间 — 跨越数据中心边界的流量（例如，仪器局域网 (LAN) - WAN - HPC 入口）。我们的解决方案可提高高达 60% 的稳定吞吐量、减少 30% 的重传和严格限制的抖动，可立即部署在运营结构上，无需硬件升级或专有扩展，并为 HPC 数据中心真正的策略驱动的端到端性能铺平道路。

papers.title: Implementing OpenMP Offload Support in the AMD Next Generation Fortran Compiler.
papers.abstract: Modern-day supercomputers are massively parallel, heterogeneous systems, many of which employ graphics processing units (GPUs) to accelerate applications. While C/C++, and also Python, gain traction in the high-performance computing (HPC) domain, Fortran continues to have a large developer base with new high-performance code written every day. The OpenMP application programming interface (API) is a key ingredient to provide multithreading and support for offloading execution to GPUs for HPC applications. To meet this need, AMD is developing the AMD Next Generation Fortran compiler (“AMD Flang”) to replace the existing “Classic Flang” compiler in the ROCm™ 7.0 release. This paper describes the general compilation pipeline of the AMD Next Generation Fortran Compiler. It shows how the compiler generates code for OpenMP target directives and their map clauses. The paper closes with a discussion of transformations in intermediate representation, such as implementing DO CONCURRENT using OpenMP intermediate code.
papers.abstract_zh: 现代超级计算机是大规模并行、异构系统，其中许多采用图形处理单元 (GPU) 来加速应用程序。虽然 C/C++ 以及 Python 在高性能计算 (HPC) 领域获得了关注，但 Fortran 仍然拥有庞大的开发人员基础，每天都会编写新的高性能代码。 OpenMP 应用程序编程接口 (API) 是为 HPC 应用程序提供多线程和支持将执行任务卸载到 GPU 的关键要素。为了满足这一需求，AMD 正在开发 AMD 下一代 Fortran 编译器（“AMD Flang”），以取代 ROCm™ 7.0 版本中现有的“Classic Flang”编译器。本文介绍了 AMD 下一代 Fortran 编译器的通用编译管道。它显示了编译器如何为 OpenMP 目标指令及其映射子句生成代码。本文最后讨论了中间表示的转换，例如使用 OpenMP 中间代码实现 DO CONCURRENT。

papers.title: Implementing support for Interactive and AI workloads in a traditional HPC environment.
papers.abstract: This paper provides an example technique for how to run interactive and AI workloads alongside traditional High-Performance computing jobs. It explains how to use short maximum runtime for all jobs and a resource limited, high priority QOS, to allow quick interactive job starts without impacting system capacity for traditional HPC jobs. It includes background on the history and culture that contributed to the implementation and technique. The reference implementation, technique, and paper are Slurm centric in terminology, but the computer scheduling concepts and methods will translate to other implementations. The paper concludes with observations on the conditions that made these techniques effective and possible areas of future work to make them more broadly applicable.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文提供了一种示例技术，说明如何与传统的高性能计算作业一起运行交互式和人工智能工作负载。它解释了如何对所有作业使用较短的最大运行时间和资源有限的高优先级 QOS，以允许快速交互式作业启动，而不影响传统 HPC 作业的系统容量。它包括有助于实施和技术的历史和文化背景。参考实现、技术和论文在术语上以 Slurm 为中心，但计算机调度概念和方法将转化为其他实现。本文最后对使这些技术有效的条件以及未来可能的工作领域进行了观察，以使它们得到更广泛的应用。

papers.title: Implications of Full-System Modeling for Superconducting Architectures.
papers.abstract: As Moore’s Law slows, superconducting electronics offer ultra-low-power, high-speed computation potential. This paper presents the first full-system superconducting architecture modeling in gem5, evaluating superconducting cores, caches, and interconnects under realistic workloads. We extend gem5 with cryogenic semiconductor (4 GHz) and superconducting (100 GHz) RISC-V cores and multi-level caches, evaluating RISC-V benchmarks and SPEC CPU2006 applications. We also integrate SRNoC, a superconducting interconnect, with the NOVA graph accelerator.
papers.abstract_zh: As Moore’s Law slows, superconducting electronics offer ultra-low-power, high-speed computation potential.本文提出了 gem5 中的第一个全系统超导架构建模，评估了实际工作负载下的超导核心、缓存和互连。我们使用低温半导体 (4 GHz) 和超导 (100 GHz) RISC-V 内核和多级缓存扩展 gem5，评估 RISC-V 基准测试和 SPEC CPU2006 应用程序。 We also integrate SRNoC, a superconducting interconnect, with the NOVA graph accelerator.

papers.title: Improving SpGEMM Performance Through Matrix-Reordering and Cluster-wise Computation.
papers.abstract: Sparse matrix-sparse matrix multiplication (SpGEMM) is a key kernel in many scientific applications and graph workloads. Unfortunately, SpGEMM is bottlenecked by data movement due to its irregular memory access patterns. Significant work has been devoted to developing row reordering schemes towards improving locality in sparse operations, but prior studies mostly focus on the case of sparse-matrix vector multiplication (SpMV).
papers.abstract_zh: 稀疏矩阵-稀疏矩阵乘法 (SpGEMM) 是许多科学应用和图形工作负载中的关键内核。不幸的是，由于其不规则的内存访问模式，SpGEMM 受到数据移动的瓶颈。人们致力于开发行重新排序方案以改善稀疏运算中的局部性，但先前的研究主要集中在稀疏矩阵向量乘法（SpMV）的情况。

papers.title: Improving Supercomputer Usage with Aging Awareness.
papers.abstract: Lifetime of electronic devices has a critical impact on their environmental footprint. In addition, the high-demand by AI companies of GPU has reduced tremendously their availability for supercomputing centers. Consequently, improving the duration of CPUs and GPUs is becoming a major issue in High Performance Computing (HPC) domain.
papers.abstract_zh: 电子设备的使用寿命对其环境足迹有着至关重要的影响。此外，人工智能公司对GPU的高需求也大大降低了超级计算中心的可用性。因此，提高CPU和GPU的持续时间正在成为高性能计算（HPC）领域的一个主要问题。

papers.title: In-Transit Data Transport Strategies for Coupled AI-Simulation Workflow Patterns.
papers.abstract: Coupled AI-Simulation workflows are becoming the major workloads for HPC facilities, and their increasing complexity necessitates new tools for performance analysis and prototyping of new in-situ workflows. We present SimAI-Bench, a tool designed to both prototype and evaluate these coupled workflows. In this paper, we use SimAI-Bench to benchmark the data transport performance of two common patterns on the Aurora supercomputer: a one-to-one workflow with co-located simulation and AI training instances, and a many-to-one workflow where a single AI model is trained from an ensemble of simulations. For the one-to-one pattern, our analysis shows that node-local and DragonHPC data staging strategies provide excellent performance compared Redis and Lustre file system. For the many-to-one pattern, we find that data transport becomes a dominant bottleneck as the ensemble size grows. Our evaluation reveals that file system is the optimal solution among the tested strategies for the many-to-one pattern.
papers.abstract_zh: 耦合的人工智能仿真工作流程正在成为 HPC 设施的主要工作负载，其日益增加的复杂性需要新的工具来进行性能分析和新的现场工作流程的原型设计。我们推出了 SimAI-Bench，这是一种旨在对这些耦合工作流程进行原型设计和评估的工具。在本文中，我们使用 SimAI-Bench 对 Aurora 超级计算机上两种常见模式的数据传输性能进行基准测试：模拟和 AI 训练实例位于同一位置的一对一工作流程，以及从一组模拟中训练单个 AI 模型的多对一工作流程。对于一对一模式，我们的分析表明，与 Redis 和 Lustre 文件系统相比，节点本地和 DragonHPC 数据暂存策略提供了出色的性能。对于多对一模式，我们发现随着整体规模的增长，数据传输成为主要瓶颈。我们的评估表明，文件系统是多对一模式的测试策略中的最佳解决方案。

papers.title: InferA: A Smart Assistant for Cosmological Ensemble Data.
papers.abstract: Analyzing large-scale scientific datasets presents substantial challenges due to their sheer volume, structural complexity, and the need for specialized domain knowledge. Automation tools, such as PandasAI, typically require full data ingestion and lack context of the full data structure, making them impractical as intelligent data analysis assistants for datasets at the terabyte scale. To overcome these limitations, we propose InferA, a multi-agent system that leverages large language models to enable scalable and efficient scientific data analysis. At the core of the architecture is a supervisor agent that orchestrates a team of specialized agents responsible for distinct phases of the data retrieval and analysis. The system engages interactively with users to elicit their analytical intent and confirm query objectives, ensuring alignment between user goals and system actions. To demonstrate the framework’s usability, we evaluate the system using ensemble runs from the HACC cosmology simulation which comprises several terabytes.
papers.abstract_zh: 由于大规模科学数据集的数量庞大、结构复杂以及对专业领域知识的需求，分析大规模科学数据集提出了巨大的挑战。 PandasAI 等自动化工具通常需要完整的数据摄取，并且缺乏完整数据结构的上下文，这使得它们作为 TB 级数据集的智能数据分析助手是不切实际的。为了克服这些限制，我们提出了 InferA，这是一种多智能体系统，利用大型语言模型来实现可扩展且高效的科学数据分析。该架构的核心是一个主管代理，它协调一组专门的代理，负责数据检索和分析的不同阶段。该系统与用户进行交互，以引出他们的分析意图并确认查询目标，确保用户目标和系统操作之间的一致性。为了证明该框架的可用性，我们使用 HACC 宇宙学模拟的整体运行来评估系统，该模拟包含数 TB 数据。

papers.title: InferCT: An Efficient and Generalizable Framework to Enable 3D Machine Learning for Computed Tomography.
papers.abstract: In this paper, we propose inferCT, an efficient and generalizable framework that enables 3D deep learning for computed tomography (CT) datasets during inference designed to be independent of dataset size and imaging task. Recent approaches have shown that 3D models routinely outperform their 2D counterparts when processing CT volumes as their architectures are designed to fully capture the 3D relationships within the data. However, their use in production is limited given the large GPU memory required during inference. Our baseline approach addresses this issue by partitioning CT volumes into cubic sub-volumes that fit into GPU memory and distributing them across multiple GPUs. Building on this, we introduce further vendor-agnostic optimizations, including a lock-free shared memory data structure to reduce synchronization overhead, pipeline execution to hide data prefetching and post-processing latency, and a parallel data loader to improve I/O efficiency. To evaluate the proposed framework, we consider image denoising as a use case and generate two CT datasets of size 1024x1024x1024 and 4096x4096x4096 voxels. Results on both AMD and NVIDIA GPUs show that our optimized framework achieves speedups of 1.97× and 2.32× over the baseline for the 10243 and 40963 datasets, respectively, using AMD MI300X and NVIDIA H100 GPUs, effectively reducing energy consumption during inference. For the scalability tests, experiments demonstrate strong scaling efficiencies of 89.25% and 75.75% when scaling from 1 to 4 GPUs within a single NUMA node, and from 1 to 8 GPUs across two NUMA nodes, respectively, using the 40963 dataset. These results validate the effectiveness of our approach and establish a performance baseline. Future work will build on this by extending the framework to larger, multi-node environments.
papers.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了 inferCT，这是一种高效且可泛化的框架，可在设计为独立于数据集大小和成像任务的推理过程中对计算机断层扫描 (CT) 数据集进行 3D 深度学习。最近的方法表明，在处理 CT 体积时，3D 模型通常优于 2D 模型，因为其架构旨在完全捕获数据内的 3D 关系。然而，鉴于推理过程中需要大量 GPU 内存，它们在生产中的使用受到限制。我们的基线方法通过将 CT 卷划分为适合 GPU 内存的立方体子卷并将其分布在多个 GPU 上来解决此问题。在此基础上，我们引入了进一步的与供应商无关的优化，包括用于减少同步开销的无锁共享内存数据结构、用于隐藏数据预取和后处理延迟的管道执行，以及用于提高 I/O 效率的并行数据加载器。为了评估所提出的框架，我们将图像去噪视为用例，并生成两个大小分别为 1024x1024x1024 和 4096x4096x4096 体素的 CT 数据集。 AMD 和 NVIDIA GPU 上的结果表明，使用 AMD MI300X 和 NVIDIA H100 GPU，我们的优化框架分别比 10243 和 40963 数据集的基准实现了 1.97 倍和 2.32 倍的加速，有效降低了推理过程中的能耗。对于可扩展性测试，实验表明，使用 40963 数据集，当单个 NUMA 节点内从 1 个 GPU 扩展到 4 个 GPU 以及跨两个 NUMA 节点从 1 个 GPU 扩展到 8 个 GPU 时，扩展效率分别为 89.25% 和 75.75%。这些结果验证了我们方法的有效性并建立了性能基线。未来的工作将在此基础上将框架扩展到更大的多节点环境。

papers.title: Insights from Optimizing HPL Performance on Exascale Systems: A Comparative Analysis of Panel Factorization.
papers.abstract: High performance LINPACK (HPL) remains the primary benchmark for evaluating supercomputing performance. It includes many parts with substantial internal complexity, and its performance is affected by a large number of parameters that interact in ways that are difficult to predict on large-scale heterogeneous supercomputer systems. We present a comprehensive performance analysis of HPL on Frontier, the world’s first exascale supercomputer, which achieved HPL performance of 1.35 exaflops. Through empirical parameter tuning, detailed modeling, and comparative evaluation, we uncover critical performance insights, share lessons learned, and outline best practices for effective parameter tuning on exascale systems. We introduce and evaluate two novel PDFACT strategies: a dedicated-thread (DT) variant and a GPU-based variant (GPUPDFACT) implementation using HIP cooperative groups, demonstrating that GPU-based factorization outperforms conventional CPU-based PDFACT on Frontier’s architecture. Our findings establish key performance factors for HPL on exascale systems and offer valuable guidance for future high-performance computing and benchmarking efforts.
papers.abstract_zh: 高性能 LINPACK (HPL) 仍然是评估超级计算性能的主要基准。它包括许多内部复杂性很高的部分，其性能受到大量参数的影响，这些参数以在大规模异构超级计算机系统上难以预测的方式相互作用。我们对世界上第一台百亿亿次超级计算机 Frontier 上的 HPL 进行了全面的性能分析，该计算机实现了 1.35 exaflops 的 HPL 性能。通过经验参数调整、详细建模和比较评估，我们揭示了关键的性能见解，分享经验教训，并概述了在百亿亿级系统上进行有效参数调整的最佳实践。我们介绍并评估了两种新颖的 PDFACT 策略：专用线程 (DT) 变体和使用 HIP 协作组的基于 GPU 的变体 (GPUPDFACT) 实现，证明基于 GPU 的分解在 Frontier 架构上优于传统的基于 CPU 的 PDFACT。我们的研究结果确定了百亿亿次系统上 HPL 的关键性能因素，并为未来的高性能计算和基准测试工作提供了宝贵的指导。

papers.title: Integrating and Characterizing HPC Task Runtime Systems for hybrid AI-HPC workloads.
papers.abstract: Scientific workflows increasingly involve both HPC and machine-learning tasks, combining MPI-based simulations, training, and inference in a single execution. Launchers such as Slurm’s srun constrain concurrency and throughput, making them unsuitable for dynamic and heterogeneous workloads. We present a performance study of RADICAL-Pilot (RP) integrated with Flux and Dragon, two complementary runtime systems that enable hierarchical resource management and high-throughput function execution. Using synthetic and production-scale workloads on Frontier, we characterize the task execution properties of RP across runtime configurations. RP+Flux sustains up to 930 tasks/s, and RP+Flux+Dragon exceeds 1,500 tasks/s with over 99.6% utilization. In contrast, srun peaks at 152 tasks/s and degrades with scale, with utilization below 50%. For IMPECCABLE.v2 drug discovery campaign, RP+Flux reduces makespan by 30–60% relative to srun/Slurm and increases throughput more than four times on up to 1,024. These results demonstrate hybrid runtime integration in RP as a scalable approach for hybrid AI-HPC workloads.
papers.abstract_zh: 科学工作流程越来越多地涉及 HPC 和机器学习任务，在一次执行中结合基于 MPI 的模拟、训练和推理。 Slurm 的 srun 等启动器会限制并发性和吞吐量，使其不适合动态和异构工作负载。我们提出了与 Flux 和 Dragon 集成的 RADICAL-Pilot (RP) 的性能研究，这两个互补的运行时系统可实现分层资源管理和高吞吐量函数执行。使用 Frontier 上的合成和生产规模工作负载，我们描述了 RP 跨运行时配置的任务执行属性。 RP+Flux sustains up to 930 tasks/s, and RP+Flux+Dragon exceeds 1,500 tasks/s with over 99.6% utilization.相比之下，srun 的峰值为 152 个任务/秒，并随着规模的扩大而下降，利用率低于 50%。对于 IMPECCABLE.v2 药物发现活动，相对于 srun/Slurm，RP+Flux 将完工时间缩短了 30-60%，并将吞吐量提高了四倍以上，高达 1,024。这些结果证明 RP 中的混合运行时集成是混合 AI-HPC 工作负载的可扩展方法。

papers.title: Integrating Distributed SQL Query Engines with Object-Based Computational Storage.
papers.abstract: Existing object storage systems like AWS S3 and MinIO offer only limited in-storage compute capabilities, typically restricted to simple SQL WHERE-clause filtering. Consequently, high-impact operators such as aggregation and top-N are still executed entirely at the compute layer. Recent advances in Object-based Computational Storage (OCS) enable these complex operators to run natively within storage, creating opportunities for substantial reductions in data movement and query time. To demonstrate these benefits in distributed SQL engines, we used Presto as a case study and developed the Presto-OCS connector, which analyzes execution plans to identify pushdown-eligible operators and offloads them to OCS for efficient in-storage execution. Evaluations with real-world HPC analytics queries and the TPC-H benchmark show that our approach achieves up to 4.07 × speedup and 99% data movement reduction compared to filter-only pushdown. When combined with compression techniques, our approach delivers 1.39 × speedup over compressed filter-only pushdown, demonstrating that advanced query pushdown complements existing optimizations.
papers.abstract_zh: AWS S3 和 MinIO 等现有对象存储系统仅提供有限的存储内计算功能，通常仅限于简单的 SQL WHERE 子句过滤。因此，聚合和 top-N 等高影响力运算符仍然完全在计算层执行。基于对象的计算存储 (OCS) 的最新进展使这些复杂的运算符能够在存储中本地运行，从而为大幅减少数据移动和查询时间创造了机会。为了展示分布式 SQL 引擎中的这些优势，我们使用 Presto 作为案例研究，并开发了 Presto-OCS 连接器，该连接器分析执行计划以识别符合下推条件的运算符，并将它们卸载到 OCS 以实现高效的存储内执行。对实际 HPC 分析查询和 TPC-H 基准的评估表明，与仅过滤器下推相比，我们的方法实现了高达 4.07 倍的加速和 99% 的数据移动减少。当与压缩技术相结合时，我们的方法比压缩的仅过滤器下推提供了 1.39 倍的加速，这表明高级查询下推补充了现有的优化。

papers.title: Intelligent Sampling of Extreme-Scale Turbulence Datasets for Accurate and Efficient Spatiotemporal Model Training.
papers.abstract: With the end of Moore’s law and Dennard scaling, efficient training increasingly requires rethinking data volume. Can we train better models with significantly less data via intelligent subsampling? To explore this, we develop SICKLE, a sparse intelligent curation framework for efficient learning, featuring a novel maximum entropy (MaxEnt) sampling approach, scalable training, and energy benchmarking. We compare MaxEnt with random and phase-space sampling on large direct numerical simulation (DNS) datasets of turbulence. Evaluating SICKLE at scale on Frontier, we show that subsampling as a preprocessing step can, in many cases, improve model accuracy and substantially lower energy consumption, with observed reductions of up to 38×.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着摩尔定律和登纳德缩放定律的终结，高效的训练越来越需要重新考虑数据量。我们能否通过智能二次采样以更少的数据训练更好的模型？为了探索这一点，我们开发了 SICKLE，这是一种用于高效学习的稀疏智能管理框架，具有新颖的最大熵 (MaxEnt) 采样方法、可扩展训练和能量基准测试。我们将 MaxEnt 与大型湍流直接数值模拟 (DNS) 数据集上的随机采样和相空间采样进行比较。在 Frontier 上大规模评估 SICKLE，我们发现，在许多情况下，子采样作为预处理步骤可以提高模型精度并显着降低能耗，观察到的能耗降低高达 38 倍。

papers.title: Inverse Design for Generating Initial Conditions in Scientific Simulations.
papers.abstract: We propose a conditional normalizing flow (CNF) surrogate model to solve generative, many-to-one inverse problems in scientific simulations governed by partial differential equations (PDEs) with time-evolving interactions between heterogeneous materials. We present two case studies: electrostatic potential and heat diffusion, which serve as proxy simulations for generating diverse sets of initial conditions that can reproduce an observed output state (transient or steady). Finally, we provide a comprehensive overview of the synthetic datasets, the model specification, each stage of the experimental workflow, evaluation of training performance, and uncertainty quantification for the generated samples.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种条件归一化流（CNF）代理模型，用于解决由偏微分方程（PDE）控制的科学模拟中的生成性多对一逆问题，以及异质材料之间随时间演化的相互作用。我们提出了两个案例研究：静电势和热扩散，它们用作生成不同初始条件集的代理模拟，这些初始条件集可以重现观察到的输出状态（瞬态或稳定）。最后，我们全面概述了合成数据集、模型规范、实验工作流程的每个阶段、训练性能评估以及生成样本的不确定性量化。

papers.title: Is it an HPC Workflow Assistant? Is it a Framework? It's Drona Workflow Engine.
papers.abstract: HPC resources are becoming increasingly complex, while HPC itself is becoming more popular among novice researchers across a wide range of research domains. These novice researchers often lack typical HPC skills, which results in a steep learning curve that leads to frustration and inefficient use of HPC resources. To address this, we developed Drona Workflow Engine. Drona offers an intuitive Graphical User Interface (GUI) that assists researchers in running their scientific workflows. The researcher provides the required information for their specific scientific workflow, and Drona generates all the scripts needed to run that workflow on the researcher’s behalf. For transparency and additional flexibility, Drona will display all generated scripts in a fully editable preview window, allowing the researcher to make any final adjustments as needed. Drona also provides a flexible framework for importing, creating, adapting, and sharing custom scientific workflows. Drona significantly enhances researcher productivity by abstracting the underlying HPC complexities while retaining full control over their workflows.
papers.abstract_zh: HPC 资源变得越来越复杂，而 HPC 本身也越来越受到各个研究领域的新手研究人员的欢迎。这些新手研究人员往往缺乏典型的 HPC 技能，从而导致学习曲线陡峭，从而导致 HPC 资源的使用效率低下和沮丧。为了解决这个问题，我们开发了 Drona 工作流程引擎。 Drona 提供直观的图形用户界面 (GUI)，可帮助研究人员运行其科学工作流程。研究人员提供其特定科学工作流程所需的信息，Drona 代表研究人员生成运行该工作流程所需的所有脚本。为了提高透明度和灵活性，Drona 将在完全可编辑的预览窗口中显示所有生成的脚本，允许研究人员根据需要进行任何最终调整。 Drona 还提供了一个灵活的框架，用于导入、创建、调整和共享自定义科学工作流程。 Drona 通过抽象底层 HPC 复杂性，同时保留对其工作流程的完全控制，显着提高了研究人员的工作效率。

papers.title: Is RISC-V ready for High Performance Computing? An evaluation of the Sophon SG2044.
papers.abstract: The pace of RISC-V adoption continues to grow rapidly, yet for the successes enjoyed in areas such as embedded computing, RISC-V is yet to gain ubiquity in High Performance Computing (HPC). The Sophon SG2044 is a next generation 64-core high performance CPU that has been designed for workstation and server grade workloads. Building upon the Sophon SG2042, subsystems that were a bottleneck in the previous generation have been upgraded.
papers.abstract_zh: RISC-V 的采用步伐持续快速增长，但尽管在嵌入式计算等领域取得了成功，但 RISC-V 尚未在高性能计算 (HPC) 领域普及。 Sophon SG2044 是下一代 64 核高性能 CPU，专为工作站和服务器级工作负载而设计。在 Sophon SG2042 的基础上，对上一代产品的瓶颈子系统进行了升级。

papers.title: IzhiRISC-V - a RISC-V-based Processor with Custom ISA Extension for Spiking Neuron Networks Processing with Izhikevich Neurons.
papers.abstract: Spiking Neural Network processing promises to provide high energy efficiency due to the sparsity of the spiking events. However, when realized on general-purpose hardware – such as a RISC-V processor – this promise can be undermined and overshadowed by the inefficient code, stemming from repeated usage of basic instructions for updating all the neurons in the network. One of the possible solutions to this issue is the introduction of a custom ISA extension with neuromorphic instructions for spiking neuron updating, and realizing those instructions in bespoke hardware expansion to the existing ALU. In this paper, we present the first step towards realizing a large-scale system based on the RISC-V-compliant processor called IzhiRISC-V, supporting the custom neuromorphic ISA extension.
papers.abstract_zh: 由于尖峰事件的稀疏性，尖峰神经网络处理有望提供高能效。然而，当在通用硬件（例如 RISC-V 处理器）上实现时，这一承诺可能会因重复使用基本指令来更新网络中的所有神经元而被低效代码所破坏和掩盖。此问题的可能解决方案之一是引入自定义 ISA 扩展，其中包含用于尖峰神经元更新的神经形态指令，并在现有 ALU 的定制硬件扩展中实现这些指令。在本文中，我们展示了实现基于 RISC-V 兼容处理器（称为 IzhiRISC-V）的大规模系统的第一步，该系统支持自定义神经形态 ISA 扩展。

papers.title: KAMI: Communication-Avoiding General Matrix Multiplication within a Single GPU.
papers.abstract: Efficient general matrix-matrix multiplication (GEMM) has attracted significant research attention in HPC and AI workloads. While large-scale GEMM has nearly achieved the peak floating-point performance of GPUs, substantial opportunities for optimization remain in small and batched GEMM operations.
papers.abstract_zh: 高效的通用矩阵-矩阵乘法 (GEMM) 引起了 HPC 和 AI 工作负载领域的广泛研究关注。虽然大规模 GEMM 已接近达到 GPU 的峰值浮点性能，但小批量 GEMM 操作仍然存在大量优化机会。

papers.title: KDRSolvers: Scalable, Flexible, Task-Oriented Krylov Solvers.
papers.abstract: We present KDRSolvers, a novel framework for representing sparse linear systems and implementing Krylov subspace methods on modern heterogeneous supercomputers. KDRSolvers uses dependent partitioning to uniformly represent sparse matrix storage formats as abstract maps between a matrix’s domain, range, and set of nonzero entries. This abstraction enables KDRSolvers to define universal co-partitioning operators for matrices and vectors independent of underlying storage formats, allowing changes in data partitioning strategies to automatically propagate through an application with no code modification. KDRSolvers also introduces multi-operator systems in which matrix and vector data can be ingested and processed in multiple non-contiguous pieces without data movement. Our implementation of KDRSolvers, targeting the Legion runtime system, achieves greater flexibility and competitive performance compared to PETSc and Trilinos. In experiments with up to 1,024 GPUs on the Lassen supercomputer, our implementation achieves up to a 9.6% reduction in execution time per iteration.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们提出了 KDRSolvers，这是一种新颖的框架，用于表示稀疏线性系统并在现代异构超级计算机上实现 Krylov 子空间方法。 KDRSolvers 使用相关分区将稀疏矩阵存储格式统一表示为矩阵域、范围和非零条目集之间的抽象映射。这种抽象使 KDRSolvers 能够独立于底层存储格式为矩阵和向量定义通用共同分区运算符，从而允许数据分区策略的更改自动通过应用程序传播，而无需修改代码。 KDRSolvers 还引入了多运算符系统，其中矩阵和矢量数据可以在多个不连续的片段中摄取和处理，而无需数据移动。与 PETSc 和 Trilinos 相比，我们针对 Legion 运行时系统实施的 KDRSolvers 实现了更大的灵活性和有竞争力的性能。在 Lassen 超级计算机上使用多达 1,024 个 GPU 进行的实验中，我们的实现使每次迭代的执行时间减少了 9.6%。

papers.title: Kilometer-Scale AI-Powered and Performance-Portable Earth System Model (AP3ESM) to Achieve Year-Scale Simulation Speed on Heterogeneous Supercomputers.
papers.abstract: Kilometer-scale Earth system models (ESMs) necessitate exascale supercomputers to facilitate realistic simulations of weather phenomena and climate variability over a time span ranging from days to decades. We present AP3ESM, an ultra‑high‑resolution, AI‑Powered, Performance‑Portable ESM coupling atmosphere, land surface, ocean, and sea ice components. By leveraging the performance portability features of Kokkos and OpenMP, the AP3ESM operates efficiently on two heterogeneous systems while incurring minimal development overhead. Advanced optimization techniques, such as adaptive parallel algorithms, AI-enhanced physical parameterizations, and mixed-precision computations, have been implemented to further boost the computational efficiency. Breaking the 1-km resolution barrier, AP3ESM delivers 0.85 and 1.98 simulated-years-per-day (SYPD) for the standalone atmosphere and ocean components on 34.1 million Sunway cores and 16085 GPUs, respectively; the holistic AP3ESM achieves 0.54 SYPD on 37.2 million Sunway cores. Notably, the forecast experiment successfully captures Super Typhoon Doksuri in 2023 and its associated extreme rainfall across China.
papers.abstract_zh: 公里级地球系统模型（ESM）需要亿亿级超级计算机来促进对几天到几十年的时间跨度内的天气现象和气候变化的真实模拟。我们推出 AP3ESM，这是一种超高分辨率、人工智能驱动、高性能便携式 ESM，耦合大气、陆地表面、海洋和海冰组件。通过利用 Kokkos 和 OpenMP 的性能可移植性功能，AP3ESM 在两个异构系统上高效运行，同时将开发开销降至最低。自适应并行算法、人工智能增强物理参数化和混合精度计算等先进优化技术已得到实施，以进一步提高计算效率。 AP3ESM 突破了 1 公里分辨率障碍，在 3410 万个 Sunway 核心和 16085 个 GPU 上分别为独立大气和海洋组件提供了 0.85 和 1.98 模拟年/天 (SYPD)；整体 AP3ESM 在 3720 万个 Sunway 核心上实现了 0.54 SYPD。值得注意的是，预报实验成功捕捉到了2023年超强台风“多克苏里”及其相关的中国极端降雨。

papers.title: KVMSR+UDWeave: Extreme-Scaling with Fine-grained Parallelism on the UpDown Graph Supercomputer.
papers.abstract: Programming irregular graph applications is challenging on today’s scalable supercomputers. We describe a novel programming model, KVMSR+UDWeave, that supports extreme scaling by exposing fine-grained parallelism. By enabling the expression of maximum parallelism, it opens the door for extreme scaling, even on both small and large graph problems.
papers.abstract_zh: 在当今的可扩展超级计算机上，编写不规则图形应用程序具有挑战性。我们描述了一种新颖的编程模型 KVMSR+UDWeave，它通过暴露细粒度并行性来支持极限扩展。通过启用最大并行性的表达，它为极端扩展打开了大门，即使在小型和大型图形问题上也是如此。

papers.title: LABMATE: Language Model Based Multi-Agent System to Accelerate Catalysis Experiments.
papers.abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) have become increasingly commonplace, they have been shown to be useful to domains far beyond just linguistic tasks. However, they are still unreliable to make decisions due to their potential to hallucinate, and unable to perform complex tasks like running simulations that are essential to a field like Material Science. To solve this, we introduce LABMATE (LAnguage model Based Multi-agent system to Accelerate caTalysis Experiments), a human-in-the-loop copilot framework that utilizes LLM agents to make catalysis research faster. LABMATE allows the human experts to submit simulation runs, track particle sizes, run data analysis, perform literature review, and generate potential hypothesis all in one framework, thereby expediting the research process. When evaluated on the Chemistry and Material Science segments of major benchmarks, LABMATE performs comparable to or better than most frontier LLMs, showing that in addition to accelerating the experimental process, our framework is also on par in domain knowledge compared to using a simple LLM. These results show that the system is able to significantly aid and improve the research process for human scientists. Furthermore, since the core architecture of the system is domain-agnostic, it can easily be adapted to other domains.
papers.abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型 (LLM) 变得越来越普遍，它们已被证明适用于远远超出语言任务的领域。然而，由于它们可能产生幻觉，它们做出的决策仍然不可靠，并且无法执行复杂的任务，例如运行模拟，这对于材料科学等领域至关重要。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 LABMATE（基于语言模型的多代理系统来加速催化实验），这是一种人机交互副驾驶框架，利用 LLM 代理来加快催化研究速度。 LABMATE 允许人类专家在一个框架中提交模拟运行、跟踪颗粒大小、运行数据分析、进行文献综述并生成潜在的假设，从而加快研究过程。当对主要基准的化学和材料科学部分进行评估时，LABMATE 的表现与大多数前沿法学硕士相当或更好，这表明除了加速实验过程之外，与使用简单的法学硕士相比，我们的框架在领域知识方面也达到了同等水平。这些结果表明该系统能够显着帮助和改进人类科学家的研究过程。此外，由于系统的核心架构与领域无关，因此可以轻松适应其他领域。

papers.title: LAMMPS-KOKKOS: Performance Portable Molecular Dynamics Across Exascale Architectures.
papers.abstract: Since its inception in 1995, LAMMPS has grown to be a world-class molecular dynamics code, with thousands of users, over one million lines of code, and multi-scale simulation capabilities. We discuss how LAMMPS has adapted to the modern heterogeneous computing landscape by integrating the Kokkos performance portability library into the existing C++ code. We investigate performance portability of simple pairwise, many-body reactive, and machine-learned force-field interatomic potentials. We present results on GPUs across different vendors and generations, and analyze performance trends, probing FLOPS throughput, memory bandwidths, cache capabilities, and thread-atomic operation performance. Finally, we demonstrate strong scaling on three exascale machines – OLCF Frontier, ALCF Aurora, and NNSA El Capitan – as well as on the CSCS Alps supercomputer, for the three potentials.
papers.abstract_zh: 自 1995 年推出以来，LAMMPS 已发展成为世界一流的分子动力学代码，拥有数千名用户、超过一百万行代码和多尺度模拟能力。我们讨论 LAMMPS 如何通过将 Kokkos 性能可移植库集成到现有的 C++ 代码中来适应现代异构计算环境。我们研究了简单成对、多体反应和机器学习的力场原子间势的性能可移植性。我们展示不同供应商和各代 GPU 的结果，并分析性能趋势、探测 FLOPS 吞吐量、内存带宽、缓存功能和线程原子操作性能。最后，我们在三台百亿亿次计算机（OLCF Frontier、ALCF Aurora 和 NNSA El Capitan）以及 CSCS Alps 超级计算机上展示了三种潜力的强大扩展能力。

papers.title: LangChain-Parsl: Connect Large Language Model Agents to High Performance Computing Resource.
papers.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) can improve performance in answering questions beyond their contextual understanding by running external tools, such as a calculator for arithmetics, an online query for real-time weather, et al. For scientific applications, this enables the LLM to perform and analyze simulation runs for more accurate answers. However, the increasing scale of scientific computing requires high-performance computers (HPCs), which are managed by job schedulers. In this work, we implemented Parsl to the LangChain tool calling to bridge the gap between the LLM agent and the HPC resource. Two implementations were set up and tested on a local Nvidia GPU workstation and the Polaris/ALCF HPC system. The first setup was implemented by modifying the LangChain tool calling, which converts the LangChain tool calls to Parsl functions and queues them to the Parsl workers for parallel execution. The second approach was achieved by designing a Parsl ensemble function as an LLM tool, which performed parallel tasks. With these implementations, the LLM agent workflow was prompted to run molecular dynamics simulations, with different protein structures and simulation conditions. The results show that our Parsl implementations enable parallel execution of scientific tools that invoked by LLM agents on both local GPU workstations and HPC platforms.
papers.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 可以通过运行外部工具（例如算术计算器、实时天气在线查询等）来提高回答超出其上下文理解范围的问题的性能。对于科学应用，这使得法学硕士能够执行和分析模拟运行以获得更准确的答案。然而，科学计算规模的不断扩大需要由作业调度程序管理的高性能计算机 (HPC)。在这项工作中，我们将 Parsl 实现到 LangChain 工具调用中，以弥合 LLM 代理和 HPC 资源之间的差距。在本地 Nvidia GPU 工作站和 Polaris/ALCF HPC 系统上设置并测试了两种实现。第一个设置是通过修改 LangChain 工具调用来实现的，它将 LangChain 工具调用转换为 Parsl 函数，并将它们排队到 Parsl Worker 中并行执行。第二种方法是通过将 Parsl 集成函数设计为 LLM 工具来实现的，该工具执行并行任务。通过这些实现，LLM代理工作流程被提示运行分子动力学模拟，具有不同的蛋白质结构和模拟条件。结果表明，我们的 Parsl 实现支持在本地 GPU 工作站和 HPC 平台上并行执行 LLM 代理调用的科学工具。
